content_of_post_to_comment: I am a very curious and conversational type person and I find myself going into random deep dives about random topics with AI. I don’t think there’s anything inherently wrong about being curious or using AI but I think the problem is there is no natural stopping point and I can lose a lot of time spinning wheels. I think another problem is using it for validation. I think a lot of time I unknowingly find myself using it for emotional support and I don’t want to develop this kind of dependency. I notice a lot of times instead of sitting with confusion, uncertainty, or disappointment I rush to assess/solve the situation with an AI discussion. The tricky part is I can’t just block AI because I need it for work and even if I block ChatGPT and Claude, it’s readily available on Google and even WhatsApp. It’s hard to run away from. Anyone have any tips on how to limit themselves on using AI and keep the brain from going soft? I feel like it also offers pseudo connection and displaces actual conversations I would have had with people.
comment: The part about rushing to AI instead of sitting with confusion hit home. I noticed I was treating uncertainty like a problem that needed immediate resolution, and AI makes that almost frictionless. One thing that helped was delaying the question by an hour. A surprising number of things either resolved themselves or stopped feeling important enough to ask.
